title: Delivery — Features and Releases
description: Framework §7. Features and releases are subgraphs of the What. 'Done' is a verifiable predicate, not a judgement. A release cut must be closed (no included element depends on an excluded one).

feature.description: The smallest independently valuable and verifiable slice; typically one flow over its concepts.

contains.description: The What-graph nodes (concepts, flows, read models) this feature is the subgraph of. A vertical (flow-shaped) cut, never a horizontal/layer cut.

depends_on.description: Other features/elements this one requires. Used to compute build order (topological) and the closed-cut check.

acceptance.description: PO-signed acceptance criteria. Part of feature_done.

done_predicate.default: every concept realised & passes domain-conformance; every flow realised & passes behavioural-conformance; every slot filled; every verification citing an element green; acceptance passes
done_predicate.description: feature_done(f). Verifiable, not asserted. Done is exactly as honest as the verifications are strong.

release.description: A chosen, coherent set of features that ship together; a subgraph whose cut must be closed.

cut_closed.description: release_done requires the cut be closed: no included element depends on an excluded one. Checked by query against feature depends_on.

done_predicate.default: all member features done AND release-level verifications green AND the cut is closed
done_predicate.description: release_done(r). A verdict over a composition — the upward contribution to the Two Pillars.
